Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Moon milk • Calming • Postpartum recovery

Ashwagandha Milk

A warm, spiced moon milk to help a frazzled nervous system wind down for sleep.
Makes 1 mug
Time 5 min
Caffeine none
For you · a bedtime cup
When to drink Evening — 30–60 min before bed. Warm and caffeine-free to help you settle after the day.

Why this heals you

  • Ashwagandha — an adaptogen traditionally used to calm stress tradition
  • Warm milk — comforting; supplies calcium & protein verified
  • Cinnamon & cardamom — warming, gently blood-sugar friendly tradition
  • Caffeine-free — a true wind-down cup tradition
Ingredients
1 cupMilk of choice
½ tspAshwagandha powder
pinchCeylon cinnamon
pinchCardamom
1 tspHoney (optional, to taste)
1 tspGhee (optional)
Method
1
Warm the milk

Gently heat 1 cup milk — not boiling.

2
Whisk in

Add ½ tsp ashwagandha, the cinnamon, cardamom and ghee. Whisk smooth.

3
Sweeten & sip

Simmer 2–3 min, take off the heat, stir in honey to taste. Sip warm before bed.

Just for you, Mum

A calming bedtime ritual for when your body is exhausted but your mind won’t switch off — small, warm and grounding.

Good to know
BestEvening, before bed.
NoteAshwagandha is generally calming; if breastfeeding, check with your provider first.
Why we say this
Ashwagandha’s traditional use as a calming adaptogen is honestly labelled tradition; milk’s calcium & protein are verified. Not a medical claim.

Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
